items.title: Sequential gradient boosting loop
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB
  init["F0 = mean(y)"] --> resid["compute pseudo-residuals r = -grad(loss)"]
  resid --> fitTree["fit shallow tree h_m on r"]
  fitTree --> update["F = F + eta * h_m"]
  update --> check{"m < M rounds?"}
  check -->|yes| resid
  check -->|no| predict["final prediction F(x)"]
items.caption: Each round fits a weak tree to what the ensemble still gets wrong, then adds it with learning rate eta.

items.title: Boosting vs bagging (random forest)
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  t1b["GB tree 1"] --> t2b["GB tree 2 fits residuals"]
  t2b --> t3b["GB tree 3 fits remaining error"]
  t1r["RF tree 1 bootstrap"] --> avg["RF average votes"]
  t2r["RF tree 2 bootstrap"] --> avg
  t3r["RF tree 3 bootstrap"] --> avg
items.caption: Boosting is sequential and corrective; bagging trains independent trees in parallel and aggregates them.
